segments.text: The war between the house of Saul and the house of David lasted a long time. David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker.
Sons were born to David in Hebron: His firstborn was Amnon the son of Ahinoam of Jezreel;
his second, Kileab the son of Abigail the widow of Nabal of Carmel; the third, Absalom the son of Maakah daughter of Talmai king of Geshur;
the fourth, Adonijah the son of Haggith; the fifth, Shephatiah the son of Abital;
and the sixth, Ithream the son of David's wife Eglah. These were born to David in Hebron.
Now Saul had had a concubine named Rizpah daughter of Aiah. And Ish-Bosheth said to Abner,
Why did you sleep with my father's concubine?
segments.speaker: Ish-Bosheth

segments.text: Abner was very angry because of what Ish-Bosheth said. So he answered,

Am I a dog's head—on Judah's side? This very day I am loyal to the house of your father Saul and to his family and friends. I haven't handed you over to David. Yet now you accuse me of an offense involving this woman!
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: May God deal with Abner, be it ever so severely, if I do not do for David what the Lord promised him on oath
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: and transfer the kingdom from the house of Saul and establish David's throne over Israel and Judah from Dan to Beersheba.
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: Then Abner sent messengers on his behalf to say to David,
Whose land is it? Make an agreement with me, and I will help you bring all Israel over to you.
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: Good,
I will make an agreement with you. But I demand one thing of you: Do not come into my presence unless you bring Michal daughter of Saul when you come to see me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Abner conferred with the elders of Israel and said,
For some time you have wanted to make David your king.
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: Now do it! For the Lord promised David, 'By my servant David I will rescue my people Israel from the hand of the Philistines and from the hand of all their enemies.'
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: Then Abner said to David,
Let me go at once and assemble all Israel for my lord the king, so that they may make a covenant with you, and that you may rule over all that your heart desires.
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: So David sent Abner away, and he went in peace.
So Joab went to the king and said,
What have you done? Look, Abner came to you. Why did you let him go? Now he is gone!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: You know Abner son of Ner; he came to deceive you and observe your movements and find out everything you are doing.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Later, when David heard about this, he said,
I and my kingdom are forever innocent before the Lord concerning the blood of Abner son of Ner.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May his blood fall on the head of Joab and on his whole family! May Joab's family never be without someone who has a running sore or leprosy or who leans on a crutch or who falls by the sword or who lacks food.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The king sang this lament for Abner:
Should Abner have died as the lawless die?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Your hands were not bound, your feet were not fettered. You fell as one falls before the wicked.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then the king said to his men,
Do you not realize that a commander and a great man has fallen in Israel this day?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And today, though I am the anointed king, I am weak, and these sons of Zeruiah are too strong for me. May the Lord repay the evildoer according to his evil deeds!
segments.speaker: David
